fault_hypothesis: The code on line 75 incorrectly removes the last element of the merged list if it is also the last element of both input lists. This violates the documented behavior of preserving all duplicates.

spec.expr: not (left and right and left[-1] == right[-1]) or (merged__1.count(left[-1]) == left.count(left[-1]) + right.count(left[-1]))

constraints.intent: After the pop, the count of the shared last element in the merged list must still equal the sum of its counts from the input lists.
The total number of elements should be preserved even when the last elements of both inputs are identical.
Before any final filtering, the merged list must contain the total number of elements from both inputs.
The final returned list should contain all elements from both input streams, preserving duplicates.
